denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 01/28/2026
UnitedHealthcare Choice Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W100039595          Group #: GRP-1005
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-104706
Claim #: CLM-2026-188266
Date of Service (requested): 01/08/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Northline Medical Group

Dear Plan Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 01/10/2026 for Teriparatide anabolic therapy related to Primary osteoporosis (M81.0). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Teriparatide anabolic therapy
Diagnosis (as submitted): Primary osteoporosis (M81.0)
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied: CDG-UM105 under UnitedHealthcare Coverage Determination Guideline (CDG) and applicable Medical Policy Bulletin

EXPLANATION OF DECISION

This request was processed under an incorrect benefit category (e.g., wellness/education or a non-medical support service) and is therefore not covered under the medical benefit for this plan.
Our review applied the criteria in CDG-UM105 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. UHC medical policy requires documented failure of prerequisite therapies before Teriparatide anabolic therapy. Submitted records do not establish failure of required lower-cost options for Primary osteoporosis (M81.0).

The file does not satisfy utilization-management requirements for step therapy missing. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a UnitedHealthcare medical director. Peer-to-peer review must be requested before a formal appeal is initiated for the same authorization decision.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W100039595) and reference UM-2026-104706. 

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
Clinical Operations

This determination was made by Dr. J. Smith, Medical Director.
clinical_context: The 49-year-old member has primary (age-related) osteoporosis (M81.0). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Teriparatide anabolic therapy recommended as the next appropriate step. Fragility fracture 2025; failed alendronate 18 months. Submitted documentation supports medical necessity for Teriparatide anabolic therapy in the setting of primary (age-related) osteoporosis (M81.0); the denial letter does not discuss those records or cite the objective findings in the file.
